Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The scenario describes a typical process, but we need to infer the constraints based on common sense and the descriptions. We have activities: RO, CA, RI, PI, QC, PO, GSL, D, IC, RP. Since its a typical process, each activity likely occurs exactly once per case. The scenario says it must be processed through a series of activities, implying all are mandatory.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The process flow: Receive Order first, then Check Availability, then Reserve Items, then Pick Items, then Quality Check, then Pack Order, then Generate Shipping Label, then Dispatch. Invoice Customer and Record Payment might happen after dispatch. Typically, an invoice is generated after the order is shipped. Payment comes after invoice.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Assume a linear order: RO -> CA -> RI -> PI -> QC -> PO -> GSL -> D -> IC -> RP.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always_before: set of (activity, its immediate predecessor): (CA, RO), (RI, CA), (PI, RI), (QC, PI), (PO, QC), (GSL, PO), (D, GSL), (IC, D), (RP, IC). Using immediate ones is sufficient to enforce the order by transitivity.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always_after: immediate successors: (RO, CA), (CA, RI), (RI, PI), (PI, QC), (QC, PO), (PO, GSL), (GSL, D), (D, IC), (IC, RP).

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Directly_follows: same immediate pairs as above since linear process with each once.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Never_together: empty, no mutually exclusive activities. Equivalence: empty, since activ_freq fixes all to 1, no need for equivalence pairs.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Activ_freq: each to {1}. This is a plausible Log Skeleton.
